summary: Audit all decided law and the kernel spec against the newly adopted constitution (R1-R6)

reGrounded: Proven-else-checked for D1-critical checkable facts in ALL build modes, with D1 safety itself re-grounded as an R4 shift-left instance (SCOPE-2, OP-4, OP-5, SCOPE-4; gates 'Constitution adopted 2026-07-05' item (a)): errors caught at check time or trapped, never silent; the round-3 decided core now carries constitutional weight. [Cited by all three audits]

No writer-emittable unsafe anywhere; exactly one gated fact-boundary family with per-fact obligation ledger and the 'no third state' amendment (SCOPE-2, GATE-1, LEDGER-1) -- re-grounded by R4 (nothing writer-stated trusted unchecked; silent corruption forbidden), R3 (exactly one boundary construct family), and R5 (the gated/audited trusted base is the constitution's explicit recorded human exception).

Owner ruling D0a 'AI-authored, human-approved trusted base' (LEDGER-1; gate 'Owner rulings D1a/D0a 2026-07-02') -- re-grounded by R5, which cites D0a by name as the deliberate, recorded, revisitable exception; human gate approval is the R5 mechanism working as designed, not an R1 violation.
No exceptions/unwinding; Result + trap=abort with runtime trapping as the fallback (ERR-1, EFF-4, SCOPE-4) -- re-grounded by R4 verbatim: no unwinding, no silent state corruption.

Zero implicit value-changing conversions + explicit cvt returning Result on narrowing, plus the BOUNDARY half of explicitness -- signatures, interfaces, instantiations fully explicit (TYPE-4, FN-1) -- re-grounded by R4 (lossy conversion unrepresentable) and R1 performance (N001/N002: an implicit conversion silently swaps a per-operation optimizer contract; D010 perturbs dispatch/specialization). Verified this merge: round2-type-inference.md records these as DECIDED FLOOR with both parties agreeing. Caveat carried: N001/N002/D009/D010 are not yet in the verified card set.

One numeric mode per arithmetic node (OP-1 one op per semantic-operation x mode, OP-3 per-node float mode) -- re-grounded by R1 (optimizer-visible facts at every node) and R3 (nothing overloaded, one spelling per operation).

No implicit dynamic dispatch; exhaustive, wildcard-free match as the core DISPATCH construct (ERR-2) -- re-grounded by R1 performance (D002 CONFIRMED: dyn dispatch is a carded optimization defeat; devirtualization by construction) and R4 (variant addition surfaces site-enumerated machine-readable edit lists at check time; missing arms unrepresentable). Scope caveat from audit 2: this evidence covers match-vs-dyn, NOT match-vs-if (see provisional list).

Monomorphization-only generics with explicit instantiation, round-2 'keep_essential' (FN-2) -- the cleanest case: R2 names it as its own precedent (round-2 checker-collapse argument; Go pre-1.18 natural experiment); D001 CONFIRMED in gate 'Load-bearing card verification (2026-07-02)'; R1 performance (guaranteed static specialization) and R1 AI-codegen (no inference/bound-solving in the trust path).

No GC/pervasive RC; checked ownership + explicit storage contracts with artifact-surfaced drops (STOR-1, STOR-3) -- re-grounded by R4 via the constitution's D1-as-R4-instance clause and R1 performance (F001/C004 zero-runtime-cost enforcement).

Conservative no-emit fallback where no check form exists; unproven nsw forbidden; solver may only promote performance facts, never gate acceptance (OP-4) -- re-grounded by R4 (fail closed; no silent UB channel; acceptance never rests on an unsound heuristic) and R1 (deterministic acceptance keeps the AI feedback loop consistent and fast).

Mandatory conservative FFI frames + barrier semantics; foreign-shared memory pre-declared at allocation site, compiler-inferred demotion a floor violation (spec sect. 14, STOR-1) -- re-grounded by R4 (shift-left at the trust boundary) and R1 (alias/storage facts stay optimizer-visible).

Canonical-form-only surface as PRINCIPLE -- one spelling per construct, non-canonical input a hard error (FORM-1 principle, META-1) -- re-grounded by R3 verbatim ('one way to say anything'), R1 (D2 corrected ruling 2026-07-02: irregularity, not verbosity, eats the spec budget), and R4 (drift made byte-detectable; round-2 syntax obligations: drift detection, node-path diagnostics, content-addressed caching on the D001/D004 channel). Scope: principle only -- the specific byte-format and the reject-vs-canonicalize policy are separately provisional.

Call-shaped canonical operations CORE ruling (OP-1/GRAM-6): EVIDENCE-SELECTED per round-2 syntax=replace_with_alternative (cards F003 frontend obligation soundness, N006 Alive2 proof-grade rewrite cost, F006 SPARK restriction precedent; zero performance cards attribute benefit to infix). Verified this merge: round2-syntax.md rules the core 'robust to either experimental outcome' of the still-unrun infix A/B, since any future write-path codec is additive bijective tooling over the fixed canonical core. The prefix writer-surface consequences remain provisional (see below).

Closed-world whole-program compilation (PROG-1) -- re-grounded by R1 performance (whole-program facts, closed-world monomorphization and exhaustiveness); carries the R1 feedback-loop latency watch item listed under tensions.

DIAG-1: every rejection cites exactly one rule ID + mechanical fix, deterministic byte-stable diagnostics -- re-grounded by R4 verbatim ('rule-citing diagnostics') and R1 feedback-loop speed; now constitutional law, not merely a toolchain floor.

Single machine-checked, version-pinned spec artifact under regularity invariants (META-1..META-4) -- re-grounded by R1 (in-context teachability and consistency are enumerated R1 values); META-1 is R3 restated at the spec level.

Round-4 guard 'no numeric token cap has cut authority until calibration audit + ablation' -- re-grounded by R2 verbatim (simplicity/budget is never a sufficient reason to cut); now constitutionally mandatory rather than procedural, given spec growth 3208 -> 6310 -> 6502 tokens across v0.1/v0.2.

No-shadow-spec rule, teaching pack as generated tested artifact, and META-5 spec-delta discipline (round-4 gates; calibration audit v0 baseline 3208 tokens) -- re-grounded by R1: teachability is priced and measured, not assumed.
Round-4 conservative-extension law + FORM-1 unknown-constructs-are-hard-errors -- re-grounded by R1 (consistency of the taught language) and R4 (no silent semantic drift across versions).

kernel-spec v0.1 blocking fixes (OWN-4 direction inversion, OWN-10 dangling own-param return, move-through-borrow ban, FN-4 law-discharge restriction, EFF-3 pure-elimination restriction) -- re-grounded by R4: each removed a silent-unsoundness channel; gates record each embodying positive test converted to negative.

LEX-1 lexicon policy + &uniq rename (kernel-spec v0.2 delta 2026-07-03; D3 rulings) -- re-grounded by R6, which cites LEX-1 by name (no backend-IR vocabulary at the surface; rustc noalias on/off history as backend coupling aging badly) and R1 (names label checked invariants; divergence census guards Rust-prior mismatch).

Owner ruling D1a + checker-core prototype PASSED (19/19 tests after v0.1), with the formalized-minimal-calculus and dynamic-oracle cross-check levers (spec sect. 5 PROVISIONAL banner) -- re-grounded by R4 (checker soundness bugs are themselves shift-left targets); prototype-before-ratification is the R3 evidence discipline applied correctly.
provisionalUnderR3: Loop form (GRAM-5 loop_stmt/break_stmt, GRAM-6: bare loop+break only) -- the anchor case, flagged by the constitution adoption gate entry itself (item (b), verified this merge) and by all three audits: chosen for grammar minimality; counted/structured/iterator alternatives never evaluated for AI codegen error rates or bounds-proof friendliness. Aggravating R1-performance angle: the round-1 arrays-loops gate is still prototype_needed and its leading candidate supplied A005 consumer facts (trip counts, reduction kinds, parallelism, control-flow shape) by construction, which bare loop+break does not surface; interacts with OWN-11 loop borrow/move restrictions. Backlog r3_loop_form.

Conditional form: no if/while/for; Bool as prelude enum with no boolean literals; match-only conditionals (GRAM-6, PRE-1, FORM-5) -- selected for one-arm-shape/spec-budget uniformity; the dispatch evidence (D002) does not speak to scalar boolean conditionals; the no-if rule first appears in kernel-spec-v0 with no debate, card, or gate entry behind it; no A/B of match-on-Bool True()/False() arms vs a dedicated branch form; round-4 familiarity topic still research_needed. [All three audits]

Statement-only match + helper-function conditional-initialization idiom (GRAM-7, EX-1) -- verified this merge: the spec-critique round-1 record posed exactly two candidates (value-arm '=> expr' vs statement-only) and picked statement-only to preserve one arm shape, i.e. cheapest-to-specify, the ground R3 explicitly disallows, while the same record calls conditional initialization 'the most common pattern an AI writer needs'. Time-urgent: FORM-1's one-spelling rule makes a later evidence-driven switch to expression-match a breaking canonical-form change, and EX-1 normatively bakes the idiom in. [All three audits]

Prefix-call-only writer surface of arithmetic (OP-1, GRAM-6: iadd.wrap<i32>(a,b) etc.) -- the canonical core is evidence-selected (see reGrounded), but per round2-syntax.md all AI-writer behavioral claims are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: 1.35-1.48x token proxy unpriced with real tokenizers; transcription-distance vs precedence-misbinding error rates unmeasured; training-distribution penalty admissible but unquantified; models overwhelmingly pretrained on infix. Any infix write-path codec is admissible only after the pre-registered gating A/B. R2 watch. [All three audits, reconciled]

TYPE-5 INTERIOR half -- no cross-statement inference; every let states its full mode and type -- round-2 type-inference verdict is needs_evidence (verified in gate table) and names this exact question 'the one open regime question'; the deciding redundancy-independence experiment (round2-type-inference follow-ups: correlated-annotation-noise vs checked-redundancy, O(k^2) token/cascade costs, all EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED) never ran. The D2-corrected ruling then foreclosed inference on spec-budget grounds -- a legitimate constitutional argument, but the literal 'cheapest to specify' ground R3 disqualifies as selection basis. Audits split on priority (blocking vs non-blocking confirmatory); owner ruling owed. Boundary half is NOT provisional (decided floor).

TYPE-6 no-shadowing/no-redeclaration (one uniform rule for values, regions, labels) -- verified this merge: recorded origin is verbatim 'cheapest for a canonical-form language' (spec-critique-round1-raw.json), the literal R3 disqualifier; not among the D1a checker-simplification levers; no card or experiment compares shadowing-permitted vs banned (fresh-name burden vs stale-binding confusion); uniquely, no gate entry flags it or schedules validation.

FN-5 env-struct pattern as the closures replacement -- round-2 'closures: replace_with_alternative' selected for checkability and monomorphization fit but never A/B-validated against restricted-closure candidates for AI codegen quality.

FN-3/conform contracts as the interfaces replacement -- round-2 'interfaces: needs_evidence' (verified in gate table) was never promoted per the gate table's own discipline, yet contract/conform shipped as normative spec; also listed as a live tension.

FORM-1 reject-never-canonicalize policy + FORM-2 specific byte-level choices (two-space indent, blank-line separation, inter-token spacing, one statement per line) -- the EXISTENCE of exactly one byte form is evidence-grounded (drift detection, content-addressed caching on the carded D001/D004 channel), but which byte form and reject-vs-accept-and-canonicalize were never tested for model formatting-error rates or repair round-trips; the validating follow-ups (drift-hypothesis test, caching-realization measurement, training-penalty decay) are all unrun. R1 feedback-loop tension. [All three audits]

FORM-4 no comments, doc-field only -- R5 removes only the human-readability argument; the effect of removing inline comment scaffolding (models' local reasoning anchors during generation) on codegen accuracy was never measured; the cut currently rests on spec regularity alone. R2 watch.

FORM-5 decimal-only integer literals with mandatory type suffix -- one-spelling ground is R3-consistent, but the absence of hex/binary forms for bitmask/flag code was never evaluated against AI codegen needs.

Checker envelope levers OWN-3 lexical-only regions, OWN-8 reject-when-unsure, OWN-11 loop borrow/move restrictions -- adopted as D1a feasibility levers (implementation-effort ground, owner-sanctioned), not because the completeness cut was shown cheap for AI writers; sound-program rejection rate and repair-loop convergence unmeasured. Section 5's PROVISIONAL banner covers calculus soundness only; this adds the R3 selection dimension.
GRAM-5 deref(p) / index<T>(p,i) prefix place forms -- one-spelling minimality choices with no AI-codegen evaluation on record. (Single-audit item, thinnest citation; carried at low priority.)
tensionsWithConstitution: Structural D2-R3 tension: the round-4 decided law makes the spec-delta column mandatory for every proposal (META-5), so the spec-cheapest candidate wins gate review by default -- exactly the selection basis R3 forbids ('not the cheapest to specify'). The gate discipline lacks the tie-break rule that spec-delta PRICES candidates but AI-codegen + performance evidence SELECTS them. Root cause of most provisional items above.

R2/R3 vs gate discipline -- a live breach: type-inference and interfaces both sit at needs_evidence in the round-2 table (verified this merge) and were never promoted, yet TYPE-5 bans interior inference (via D2 spec-cost reasoning) and FN-3 ships the interfaces replacement as normative. Decided without the evidence the gates required; the constitution makes this the clearest procedural violation on record.

R2 vs the metaprogramming ban while its replacement is deferred: round-2 'metaprogramming: replace_with_alternative' is law, but the alternative (closed constant-expression sublanguage) is DEFERRED -- CONST-1 admits integer literals only. R2's own cited natural experiment (Go pre-1.18: go:generate as unchecked external templating) is precisely the failure mode invited for as long as the gap persists.

R1-performance tension at the loop form: GRAM-6 iteration surfaces zero optimizer-visible loop facts, while the round-1 arrays-loops leading candidate (gate: prototype_needed) established that declared loop kinds supply the A005 consumer list by construction. The kernel froze the weakest-fact iteration form before the discriminating prototype ran -- the performance half of R1 is currently unserved by the retained form.

R3 vs ratification sequencing, with breaking-change urgency: kernel v0.2 hardened forms whose deciding experiments round 2 pre-registered but never ran (syntax A/B, redundancy-independence, drift experiments), and the current ratification preconditions (sect. 5 formal-calculus reconciliation, sect. 9 effect carding, owner counting rulings) exclude these R3 debts. Because FORM-1 admits exactly one spelling per construct, a later evidence-driven switch (e.g., GRAM-7 to expression-match; EX-1 bakes the helper idiom) is a BREAKING canonical-form change -- the R3 audit is time-urgent, not backlog.

R1 (feedback-loop speed) vs PROG-1 closed world with no separate compilation plus FN-2 re-checking every monomorphized instantiation as concrete code: check-loop latency is an enumerated R1 value, but no gate entry measures or budgets whole-program recompile cost at target scale.

R1 (feedback-loop speed) vs FORM-1's toolchain-never-auto-formats hard error: every byte-level formatting deviation costs a full generate-reject-regenerate round trip; an accept-and-canonicalize alternative was never evaluated.

R4 (trapping is the fallback) vs round-3 'wrap fallback legal' wording for overflow: silent wraparound is a silent-logic-error channel. Currently defused because OP-1 has no default (writer names .wrap/.trap/.checked per node; OP-2 excludes wrap where unsound), but any future defaulting rule instantiating 'wrap fallback' would violate R4 and META-2 simultaneously; the round-3 wording should be annotated now.

R6 vs the undecided ir-strategy verdict: round-1 'ir-strategy: research_needed' is still open while the normative operation table (OP-1) and artifact rules (DIAG-2 'acceptance decidable from the artifact alone') accrete; only the lexicon (LEX-1) has been audited for backend coupling -- the v0.2 Rust-divergence audit does not cover operation- or artifact-level coupling.

D2 budget vs R4-load-bearing diagnostics: the no-shadow-spec rule taxes precisely what R4 prioritizes -- DIAG-3 report field schemas and ERR-3 verifier-checked Result propagation + Result-vs-trap classification were deferred as counted spec mass, yet under R4 + R1 the check/lifetime/trap report family and the classification rule are load-bearing for keeping recoverable errors shift-left, not deferrable luxuries.

D1a-R2 tension at the checker levers: OWN-3 lexical regions and OWN-8 reject-when-unsure are grounded in the D1a effort ceiling, but under R2 a checker cut that inflates rejections of sound programs harms AI codegen; rejection-rate / restructuring-churn measurement should accompany the sect. 5 formal-calculus reconciliation its PROVISIONAL marker already requires.

R1-unaccounted human-residue rules (audit flags): (a) FORM-2 indentation/blank-line/spacing conventions encode information already carried by braces and separators -- training-distribution-alignment defense exists but is uncarded; (b) FORM-4/GRAM-3 doc STRING field -- human-readable prose in a language whose text humans are declared not to read (R5), its only defensible grounding (writer re-read context, R5 gate-audit support at LEDGER-1 signatures) nowhere recorded; (c) free-text message in GRAM-4 check_stmt / SCOPE-4 trap reports, where DIAG-3 already mandates rule ID + node path. None load-bearing for soundness; silent human-serving rules are the one thing R1+R5 jointly forbid. Related R2 near-miss note: GRAM-7, no-if, and TYPE-6 are the most plausible R2-reversal sites if experiments land badly -- the same cut-for-simplicity move as the generics case, made WITHOUT a checker-collapse-grade argument.
newObligations: Build the AI-codegen validation harness R3 presupposes: no instrument in the record measures first-parse success, rule-violation rate, repair-loop iterations, or provability outcomes for generated code (implementation/validation-harness-plan.md targets optimizer soundness only). Blocks ratification of every provisionalUnderR3 item; executes backlog entries r3_audit and r3_loop_form.

Promote the already-pre-registered but unrun round-2 experiments to blocking R3 validation gates in decision-gates.md: (a) syntax A/B with real tokenizers and whole programs (round2-syntax follow-ups -- validates the OP-1/GRAM-6 writer surface, prices the 1.35-1.48x proxy, measures operand-order vs precedence-misbinding error rates and repair iterations); (b) redundancy-independence experiment (round2-type-inference follow-ups -- decides TYPE-5 interior annotation); (c) drift-hypothesis and caching-realization measurements (validate FORM-1/FORM-2); (d) flagship drift experiment (round2-synthesis -- underwrites FN-2's anti-drift claim).

Register the R3 selection experiments that exist nowhere today: (a) loop-form evaluation -- loop+break vs counted/structured/reduction forms, measuring AI error rates AND provability of trip-count/independence facts, folded into the still-open arrays-loops prototype gate; (b) conditional-form A/B -- match-on-Bool vs a dedicated two-arm branch, plus helper-fn vs value-arm conditional-initialization idiom cost (GRAM-6/GRAM-7/PRE-1/EX-1); (c) shadowing on/off for TYPE-6, or re-ground it as a D1a checker lever with evidence in notes/checker-feasibility-findings; (d) FN-5 env-struct vs restricted closures; (e) FORM-4 doc-only vs comment scaffolding; (f) FORM-5 decimal-only vs hex/binary literals.

Extend the R3-provisional register beyond the loop form AND mark it in the spec text: kernel-spec v0.2 carries PROVISIONAL banners only on sect. 5 and sect. 9; add equivalent visible banners with META-5 spec-delta declarations and ratification preconditions to GRAM-7, TYPE-6, GRAM-6/PRE-1 conditionals, TYPE-5 interior half, FN-3, FN-5, FORM-1/2/4/5, the OP-1 prefix writer surface, and GRAM-5 place forms.

Amend gate discipline to resolve the structural D2-R3 tension, operationalized as a META-5 extension: every future spec delta declares its selection ground -- evidence-selected vs minimality-selected -- alongside the existing rules/tokens/spellings/exceptions columns; minimality-selected forms auto-enter the PROVISIONAL register; record that the spec-delta column prices but never selects. Fold into the pending owner rulings on counting procedure and D2 binding unit.

Amend the ratification-preconditions list (currently sect. 5 formal-calculus reconciliation, sect. 9 effect carding, owner counting rulings) to include the R3 validation debts above, so no provisional form can ratify by default -- urgent because FORM-1 makes later form changes breaking.

Back-fill the two foreclosed round-2 needs_evidence verdicts so gate table and shipped spec stop contradicting each other: (a) interfaces -- under R2 the burden flips to 'prove contract/conform + env-struct (FN-3, FN-5) does not harm AI codegen relative to richer interface forms' with Go interface{}-boxing as the reference failure mode; promote from backlog to active audit; (b) type-inference interior -- run the deciding redundancy-independence experiment or record an explicit owner ruling superseding the gate requirement (the merged audits split on blocking vs non-blocking-confirmatory priority; the ruling should settle it).

R2 discharge for metaprogramming: deliver the constant-expression sublanguage (CONST-1 DEFERRED delta) or produce evidence AI writers need no generation-time abstraction beyond monomorphized generics; add a monitor for external-templating emergence in any writer corpus (the go:generate signature R2 cites).

R4 reprioritization of the diagnostics/report family: promote the DEFERRED items -- DIAG-3 report field schemas, ERR-3 Result propagation and Result-vs-trap classification rule, FN-5 env-struct exactness diagnostics and typed operation tables, OP-5 stated-and-checked vocabulary -- from recorded-delta backlog to next-priced META-5 spec deltas; promote round-4 compiler-as-teacher from research_needed to active (R1 feedback-loop speed + R4).

R6 backend-coupling audit: resolve the pending ir-strategy verdict (round-1 research_needed since 2026-07-01; now the highest-priority open round-1 topic -- R6's text says 'ir-strategy verdict pending'); extend the LEX-1 divergence census beyond lexicon to the OP-1 operation table (mode vocabulary, trap semantics) and the DIAG-2 elaborated artifact format; add backend/ISA-neutrality as an explicit promotion requirement for any IR design_candidate.

R1 loop-latency budget: define and measure a check-loop latency target for closed-world compilation (PROG-1) including FN-2 instantiation re-checking at representative program scale; record it as a gate the same way spec tokens are gated by the calibration audit.

Close the card-verification debt under the forms that ARE evidence-selected: N001/N002 (grounds OP-1 mode-suffixed opnames and TYPE-4) and D009/D010 (flagged 'remaining unverified' in the load-bearing card-verification gate) must pass adversarial verification before sect. 7/sect. 4 ratification, plus the rest of the A/D/C/J series (backlog round2_empirical) -- R3's 'survivor chosen by evidence' is only as strong as the verified card base under it.

Run an R1 justification pass over the human-residue rules: FORM-2 formatting conventions, FORM-4 doc field, and check/trap free-text message strings -- for each, either card an R1 grounding (training-distribution alignment, writer re-read context, AI repair-loop signal) or record it as an explicit R5 exception in notes/user-directives.md, as R5 requires.

R5 revisit trigger: schedule the gate-efficacy experiments that D0a names as its revisit condition and record the owning trigger entry in decision-gates.md -- R5 marks the human-approval exception 'revisitable as verification tooling matures', which currently has no owning entry.

Re-scope the round-4 familiarity topic: under R3, familiarity is inadmissible as a selection ground; the research question narrows to measuring familiarity's causal effect on AI error rates as evidence input only. Secondary R6 reprioritization: revisit round-1 static-vs-profile (research_needed) -- the self-improving-compiler horizon in R6 makes profile feedback a stack-level design input rather than an optimization detail, raising it above the remaining round-1 backlog.
